description_long: This is a two-story house with a weathered blue stucco exterior, featuring patches of exposed brick and stonework that add a rustic charm. The roof is covered with reddish-orange terracotta tiles, and a prominent chimney rises from one side. The front facade includes a wooden door, several shuttered windows, and a small circular attic window, while the side reveals an external staircase leading to a second-floor arched doorway and a covered balcony supported by wooden posts. The house also has a small balcony with a balustrade and a covered porch area, both accessible from the upper floor. Architectural details such as stone corner accents, wooden window frames, and a mix of rectangular and arched windows contribute to its Mediterranean character. The overall appearance suggests an older, lived-in residence typical of southern European urban neighborhoods.
description: A weathered two-story blue house with terracotta roof tiles, exposed brick patches, wooden shutters, and a prominent external staircase. Features include balconies, a covered porch, and rustic Mediterranean architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main wooden door, two shuttered windows, a circular attic window, and visible weathering on the blue facade.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the covered porch with terracotta tiles, wooden supports, and the external staircase leading up to the second floor.
description_view_2: Rear/side view displaying the full external staircase, arched doorway, exposed brickwork, and a small arched window.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a small balcony, two arched windows, and significant weathering on the blue wall.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with a mix of rectangular and arched windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry features a wooden door with stone steps, flanked by shuttered windows and a small overhang; external staircase and covered porch add to the entry complexity.
